extraction.one_line_summary: GROVES Kaden won the sprint finale on Melbourne city circuits after teammate SMITH Dion led out, taking his second stage victory of the tour.
tactical_patterns: VINE Jay exploited intermediate sprints to leapfrog GC positions, securing fifth overall without contesting final sprint or breakaways.
Team Mitchelton's dual-threat sprint setup let SMITH Dion lead out then celebrate second while GROVES still won dominantly.
HILL Ben's daily breakaway participation mathematically locked points jersey without stage wins, showing volume strategy over peak performance.
Breakaway on fast criterium circuits stalled at 35 seconds for final 30km, suggesting crit racing makes time gaps unsustainable.

rider_signals.VINE Jay: positive: Gaming intermediate sprints for GC placings reveals tactical maturity beyond just following wheels in finale
rider_signals.DAINESE Alberto: negative: Getting caught 50th in position on predictable criterium sprint finish exposes positioning weakness in technical circuits
rider_signals.BERWICK Sean: negative: Caught wrong side of split on final stage despite holding second overall suggests late-race concentration lapse
rider_signals.SMITH Dion: positive: Had enough margin to celebrate before line while finishing second indicates team sprint dominance was overwhelming
